content.text:
Liora (Jan 2026 - Mar 2026)
Weather forecast in Australia MLOps project (Training program)

content.html:
Overview - What the Project Is

This project implements a full end-to-end MLOps pipeline for predicting RainTomorrow (binary rainfall prediction) using 10 years of Australian weather data. It demonstrates how to design, orchestrate, deploy, and monitor a production-ready ML system using modern DevOps and MLOps practices.

Purpose - Why the Project Matters

Shows the ability to build scalable, automated ML workflows instead of static notebooks. Demonstrates production-grade engineering skills: microservices, orchestration, monitoring, CI/CD thinking. Solves a real-world supervised classification problem with a rich meteorological dataset. Highlights experience with data pipelines, model lifecycle management, and service-based architectures.

items: Data Ingestion: Pull latest weather data and merge with historical dataset.
Preprocessing: Handle missing values, encode categorical features, generate model-ready features.
Model Selection: 5-fold cross-validation to choose best hyperparameters.
Model Training: Train final model and evaluate accuracy + F-metrics.
Model Serving: Expose prediction endpoint via microservice.
API Gateway: Single entry point for all requests → FastAPI / Nginx (depending on repo setup)
Data Ingestion Service: Fetches and appends new raw data → Python, Pandas, Requests
Data Processing Service: Cleans, encodes, and feature-engineers → Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn
Model Training Service: Cross-validation, hyperparameter tuning, training → Scikit-learn
Inference Service: Serves predictions via API → FastAPI, Pickle/Joblib
Monitoring Metrics: Collection and dashboards → Prometheus + Grafana
